          },
          {
            "text": "        2 modes are available for instantiating this class, depending on 2 different use cases:",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 587
          },
          {
            "text": "        1. Your dataset already exists:",

          },
          {

          {

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 600
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 601
          },
          {
            "text": "        2. Your dataset doesn't already exists (either on local disk or on the Hub): you can create an empty",
            "lineNumber": 602
          },
          {
            "text": "           LeRobotDataset with the 'create' classmethod. This can be used for recording a dataset or port an",

          },
          {
            "text": "           existing dataset to the LeRobotDataset format.",
            "lineNumber": 604
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 605
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 606
          },
          {
            "text": "        In terms of files, LeRobotDataset encapsulates 3 main things:",

            "text": "            - metadata:",

          },
          {
            "text": "                - info contains various information about the dataset like shapes, keys, fps etc.",

          },
          {

          },

            "text": "            │   └── ...",

          },
          {
            "text": "            └── ...",
            "lineNumber": 657
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 658
          },
          {
            "text": "        Note that this file-based structure is designed to be as versatile as possible. Multiple episodes are",
            "lineNumber": 659
          },
          {
            "text": "        consolidated into chunked files which improves storage efficiency and loading performance. The",
            "lineNumber": 660
          },
          {
            "text": "        structure of the dataset is entirely described in the info.json file, which can be easily downloaded",
            "lineNumber": 661
          },
          {
            "text": "        or viewed directly on the hub before downloading any actual data. The type of files used are very",
            "lineNumber": 662
          },
          {
            "text": "        simple and do not need complex tools to be read, it only uses .parquet, .json and .mp4 files (and .md",
            "lineNumber": 663
          },
          {
            "text": "        for the README).",
            "lineNumber": 664
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 665
          },
          {
            "text": "        Args:",
            "lineNumber": 666
          },
          {
            "text": "            repo_id (str): This is the repo id that will be used to fetch the dataset.",
            "lineNumber": 667
          },
          {
            "text": "            root (Path | None, optional): Local directory where the dataset will be downloaded and",
            "lineNumber": 668
          },
          {
            "text": "                stored. If set, all dataset files will be stored directly under this path. If not set, the",
            "lineNumber": 669
          },
          {
            "text": "                dataset files will be stored under $HF_LEROBOT_HOME/repo_id (configurable via the",
            "lineNumber": 670
          },
          {
            "text": "                HF_LEROBOT_HOME environment variable).",
            "lineNumber": 671
          },
          {
            "text": "            episodes (list[int] | None, optional): If specified, this will only load episodes specified by",
            "lineNumber": 672
          },
          {
            "text": "                their episode_index in this list. Defaults to None.",
            "lineNumber": 673
          },
          {
            "text": "            image_transforms (Callable | None, optional): You can pass standard v2 image transforms from",
            "lineNumber": 674
          },
          {
            "text": "                torchvision.transforms.v2 here which will be applied to visual modalities (whether they come",
            "lineNumber": 675
          },
          {
            "text": "                from videos or images). Defaults to None.",
            "lineNumber": 676
          },
          {
            "text": "            delta_timestamps (dict[list[float]] | None, optional): _description_. Defaults to None.",
            "lineNumber": 677
          },
          {
            "text": "            tolerance_s (float, optional): Tolerance in seconds used to ensure data timestamps are actually in",
            "lineNumber": 678
          },
          {
            "text": "                sync with the fps value. It is used at the init of the dataset to make sure that each",
            "lineNumber": 679
          },
          {
            "text": "                timestamps is separated to the next by 1/fps +/- tolerance_s. This also applies to frames",
            "lineNumber": 680
          },
          {
            "text": "                decoded from video files. It is also used to check that &#96;delta_timestamps&#96; (when provided) are",
            "lineNumber": 681
          },
          {
            "text": "                multiples of 1/fps. Defaults to 1e-4.",
            "lineNumber": 682
          },
          {
            "text": "            revision (str, optional): An optional Git revision id which can be a branch name, a tag, or a",
            "lineNumber": 683
          },
          {
            "text": "                commit hash. Defaults to current codebase version tag.",
            "lineNumber": 684
          },
          {
            "text": "            force_cache_sync (bool, optional): Flag to sync and refresh local files first. If True and files",
            "lineNumber": 685
          },
          {
            "text": "                are already present in the local cache, this will be faster. However, files loaded might not",
            "lineNumber": 686
          },
          {
            "text": "                be in sync with the version on the hub, especially if you specified 'revision'. Defaults to",
            "lineNumber": 687
          },
          {
            "text": "                False.",
            "lineNumber": 688
          },
          {
            "text": "            download_videos (bool, optional): Flag to download the videos. Note that when set to True but the",
            "lineNumber": 689
          },
          {
            "text": "                video files are already present on local disk, they won't be downloaded again. Defaults to",
            "lineNumber": 690
          },
          {
            "text": "                True.",
            "lineNumber": 691
          },
          {
            "text": "            video_backend (str | None, optional): Video backend to use for decoding videos. Defaults to torchcodec when available int the platform; otherwise, defaults to 'pyav'.",
            "lineNumber": 692
          },
          {
            "text": "                You can also use the 'pyav' decoder used by Torchvision, which used to be the default option, or 'video_reader' which is another decoder of Torchvision.",
            "lineNumber": 693
          },
          {
            "text": "            batch_encoding_size (int, optional): Number of episodes to accumulate before batch encoding videos.",
            "lineNumber": 694
          },
          {
            "text": "                Set to 1 for immediate encoding (default), or higher for batched encoding. Defaults to 1.",
            "lineNumber": 695
          },
          {
            "text": "            vcodec (str, optional): Video codec for encoding videos during recording. Options: 'h264', 'hevc',",
            "lineNumber": 696
          },
          {
            "text": "                'libsvtav1', 'auto', or hardware-specific codecs like 'h264_videotoolbox', 'h264_nvenc'.",
            "lineNumber": 697
          },
          {
            "text": "                Defaults to 'libsvtav1'. Use 'auto' to auto-detect the best available hardware encoder.",
            "lineNumber": 698
          },
          {
            "text": "            streaming_encoding (bool, optional): If True, encode video frames in real-time during capture",
            "lineNumber": 699
          },
          {
            "text": "                instead of writing PNG images first. This makes save_episode() near-instant. Defaults to False.",
            "lineNumber": 700
          },
          {
            "text": "            encoder_queue_maxsize (int, optional): Maximum number of frames to buffer per camera when using",
            "lineNumber": 701
          },
          {
            "text": "                streaming encoding. Defaults to 30 (~1s at 30fps).",

        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "def convert_dataset(",

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 459,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    if data_file_size_in_mb is None:",
            "lineNumber": 460
          },
          {
            "text": "        data_file_size_in_mb = DEFAULT_DATA_FILE_SIZE_IN_MB",
            "lineNumber": 461
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "text": "        video_file_size_in_mb = DEFAULT_VIDEO_FILE_SIZE_IN_MB",
            "lineNumber": 463
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 464
          },
          {
            "text": "    # First check if the dataset already has a v3.0 version",
            "lineNumber": 465
          },
          {
            "text": "    if root is None and not force_conversion:",
            "lineNumber": 466
          },
          {
            "text": "        try:",
            "lineNumber": 467
          },
          {
            "text": "            print(\"Trying to download v3.0 version of the dataset from the hub...\")",
            "lineNumber": 468
          },
          {
            "text": "            snapshot_download(repo_id, repo_type=\"dataset\", revision=V30, local_dir=HF_LEROBOT_HOME / repo_id)",
            "lineNumber": 469
          },
          {
            "text": "            return",
            "lineNumber": 470
          },
          {
            "text": "        except Exception:",
            "lineNumber": 471
          },
          {
            "text": "            print(\"Dataset does not have an uploaded v3.0 version. Continuing with conversion.\")",
            "lineNumber": 472
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 473
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Set root based on whether local dataset path is provided",
            "lineNumber": 474
          },
          {
            "text": "    use_local_dataset = False",
            "lineNumber": 475
          },
          {
            "text": "    root = HF_LEROBOT_HOME / repo_id if root is None else Path(root)",
            "lineNumber": 476
          },
          {
            "text": "    if root.exists():",
            "lineNumber": 477
          },
          {
            "text": "        validate_local_dataset_version(root)",
            "lineNumber": 478
          },
          {
            "text": "        use_local_dataset = True",
            "lineNumber": 479
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(f\"Using local dataset at {root}\")",
            "lineNumber": 480
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 481
          },
          {
            "text": "    old_root = root.parent / f\"{root.name}_old\"",
            "lineNumber": 482
          },
          {
            "text": "    new_root = root.parent / f\"{root.name}_v30\"",
            "lineNumber": 483
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 484
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Handle old_root cleanup if both old_root and root exist",
            "lineNumber": 485
          },
          {
            "text": "    if old_root.is_dir() and root.is_dir():",
            "lineNumber": 486
          },
          {
            "text": "        shutil.rmtree(str(root))",
            "lineNumber": 487
          },
          {
            "text": "        shutil.move(str(old_root), str(root))",
            "lineNumber": 488
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 489
          },
          {
            "text": "    if new_root.is_dir():",
            "lineNumber": 490
          },
          {
            "text": "        shutil.rmtree(new_root)",
            "lineNumber": 491
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 492
          },
          {
            "text": "    if not use_local_dataset:",
            "lineNumber": 493
          },
          {
            "text": "        snapshot_download(",
            "lineNumber": 494
          },
          {
            "text": "            repo_id,",
            "lineNumber": 495
          },
          {
            "text": "            repo_type=\"dataset\",",
            "lineNumber": 496
          },
          {
            "text": "            revision=V21,",
            "lineNumber": 497
          },
          {
            "text": "            local_dir=root,",
            "lineNumber": 498
          },
          {
            "text": "        )",
            "lineNumber": 499
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 500
          },
          {
            "text": "    convert_info(root, new_root, data_file_size_in_mb, video_file_size_in_mb)",
            "lineNumber": 501
          },
          {
            "text": "    convert_tasks(root, new_root)",
            "lineNumber": 502
          },
          {
            "text": "    episodes_metadata = convert_data(root, new_root, data_file_size_in_mb)",
            "lineNumber": 503
          },
          {
            "text": "    episodes_videos_metadata = convert_videos(root, new_root, video_file_size_in_mb)",
            "lineNumber": 504
          },
          {
            "text": "    convert_episodes_metadata(root, new_root, episodes_metadata,",

          }
        ]

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/lerobot/datasets/v30/convert_dataset_v21_to_v30.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 64,
            "column": 23
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "#!/usr/bin/env python",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "# Copyright 2025 The HuggingFace Inc. team. All rights reserved.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "#",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "# You may obtain a copy of the License at",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "#",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "#",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "# distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "# limitations under the License.",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "This script will help you convert any LeRobot dataset already pushed to the hub from codebase version 2.1 to",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "3.0. It will:",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "- Generate per-episodes stats and writes them in &#96;episodes_stats.jsonl&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "- Check consistency between these new stats and the old ones.",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "- Remove the deprecated &#96;stats.json&#96;.",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "- Update codebase_version in &#96;info.json&#96;.",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "- Push this new version to the hub on the 'main' branch and tags it with \"v3.0\".",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "Usage:",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "Convert a dataset from the hub:",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "python src/lerobot/datasets/v30/convert_dataset_v21_to_v30.py \\",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "    --repo-id=lerobot/pusht",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "Convert a local dataset (works in place):",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "python src/lerobot/datasets/v30/convert_dataset_v21_to_v30.py \\",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "    --repo-id=lerobot/pusht \\",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "    --root=/path/to/local/dataset/directory \\",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "    --push-to-hub=false",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "N.B. Path semantics (v2): --root is the exact dataset folder containing",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "meta/, data/, videos/. When omitted, defaults to $HF_LEROBOT_HOME/{repo_id}.",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "import argparse",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "import logging",
            "lineNumber": 49

            "text": "# limitations under the License.",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "This file contains lists of available environments, dataset and policies to reflect the current state of LeRobot library.",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "We do not want to import all the dependencies, but instead we keep it lightweight to ensure fast access to these variables.",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "Example:",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "    &#96;&#96;&#96;python",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "        import lerobot",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(lerobot.available_envs)",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(lerobot.available_tasks_per_env)",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(lerobot.available_datasets)",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(lerobot.available_datasets_per_env)",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(lerobot.available_real_world_datasets)",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(lerobot.available_policies)",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(lerobot.available_policies_per_env)",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(lerobot.available_robots)",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(lerobot.available_cameras)",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "        print(lerobot.available_motors)",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "    &#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "When implementing a new dataset loadable with LeRobotDataset follow these steps:",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "- Update &#96;available_datasets_per_env&#96; in &#96;lerobot/__init__.py&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "When implementing a new environment (e.g. &#96;gym_aloha&#96;), follow these steps:",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "- Update &#96;available_tasks_per_env&#96; and &#96;available_datasets_per_env&#96; in &#96;lerobot/__init__.py&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },

            "text": "When implementing a new policy class (e.g. &#96;DiffusionPolicy&#96;) follow these steps:",

          },
          {
            "text": "- Update &#96;available_policies&#96; and &#96;available_policies_per_env&#96;, in &#96;lerobot/__init__.py&#96;",

            "text": "- Set the required &#96;name&#96; class attribute.",

            "lineNumber": 44

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48

          {

          }

          {
            "text": "def hw_to_dataset_features(",
            "lineNumber": 615,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    hw_features: dict[str, type | tuple], prefix: str, use_video: bool = True",
            "lineNumber": 616,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": ") -&gt; dict[str, dict]:",
            "lineNumber": 617,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Convert hardware-specific features to a LeRobot dataset feature dictionary.",
            "lineNumber": 618
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 619
          },
          {
            "text": "    This function takes a dictionary describing hardware outputs (like joint states",
            "lineNumber": 620
          },
          {
            "text": "    or camera image shapes) and formats it into the standard LeRobot feature",
            "lineNumber": 621
          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 623
          },
          {
            "text": "    Args:",

          },
          {
            "text": "        hw_features (dict): Dictionary mapping feature names to their type (float for",

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 626
          },

            "lineNumber": 627
          },
          {
            "text": "            or \"action\").",
            "lineNumber": 628
          },
          {
            "text": "        use_video (bool): If True, image features are marked as \"video\", otherwise \"image\".",
            "lineNumber": 629
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 630
          },
          {
            "text": "    Returns:",
            "lineNumber": 631
          },
          {
            "text": "        dict: A LeRobot features dictionary.",
            "lineNumber": 632
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 633
          },
          {
            "text": "    features = {}",
            "lineNumber": 634
          },
          {
            "text": "    joint_fts = {",
            "lineNumber": 635
          },
          {
            "text": "        key: ftype",
            "lineNumber": 636
          },
          {
            "text": "        for key, ftype in hw_features.items()",
            "lineNumber": 637
          },
          {
            "text": "        if ftype is float or (isinstance(ftype, PolicyFeature) and ftype.type != FeatureType.VISUAL)",
            "lineNumber": 638
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 639
          },
          {
            "text": "    cam_fts = {key: shape for key, shape in hw_features.items() if isinstance(shape, tuple)}",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 641
          },
          {
            "text": "    if joint_fts and prefix == ACTION:",

          },
          {
            "text": "        features[prefix] = {",

          },
          {
            "text": "            \"dtype\": \"float32\",",

          },
          {
            "text": "            \"shape\": (len(joint_fts),),",
            "lineNumber": 645
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"names\": list(joint_fts),",
            "lineNumber": 646
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 648
          },
          {
            "text": "    if joint_fts and prefix == OBS_STR:",
            "lineNumber": 649
          },
          {

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 651
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "text": "            \"names\": list(joint_fts),",
            "lineNumber": 653
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 654
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 655
          },
          {
            "text": "    for key, shape in cam_fts.items():",
            "lineNumber": 656
          },
          {
            "text": "        features[f\"{prefix}.images.{key}\"] = {",

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 658
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"shape\": shape,",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 662

          {
            "text": "def aggregate_datasets(",
            "lineNumber": 233,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    repo_ids: list[str],",
            "lineNumber": 234,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    aggr_repo_id: str,",
            "lineNumber": 235,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    roots: list[Path] | None = None,",
            "lineNumber": 236,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    aggr_root: Path | None = None,",
            "lineNumber": 237,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    data_files_size_in_mb: float | None = None,",
            "lineNumber": 238,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    video_files_size_in_mb: float | None = None,",
            "lineNumber": 239,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    chunk_size: int | None = None,",
            "lineNumber": 240,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "):",
            "lineNumber": 241,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Aggregates multiple LeRobot datasets into a single unified dataset.",
            "lineNumber": 242
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 243
          },
          {
            "text": "    This is the main function that orchestrates the aggregation process by:",
            "lineNumber": 244
          },
          {
            "text": "    1. Loading and validating all source dataset metadata",
            "lineNumber": 245
          },
          {
            "text": "    2. Creating a new destination dataset with unified tasks",
            "lineNumber": 246
          },
          {
            "text": "    3. Aggregating videos, data, and metadata from all source datasets",
            "lineNumber": 247
          },
          {
            "text": "    4. Finalizing the aggregated dataset with proper statistics",
            "lineNumber": 248
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 249
          },
          {
            "text": "    Args:",
            "lineNumber": 250
          },
          {
            "text": "        repo_ids: List of repository IDs for the datasets to aggregate.",
            "lineNumber": 251
          },
          {
            "text": "        aggr_repo_id: Repository ID for the aggregated output dataset.",
            "lineNumber": 252
          },
          {
            "text": "        roots: Optional list of root paths for the source datasets.",
            "lineNumber": 253
          },
          {
            "text": "        aggr_root: Optional root path for the aggregated dataset.",
            "lineNumber": 254
          },
          {
            "text": "        data_files_size_in_mb: Maximum size for data files in MB (defaults to DEFAULT_DATA_FILE_SIZE_IN_MB)",
            "lineNumber": 255
          },
          {
            "text": "        video_files_size_in_mb: Maximum size for video files in MB (defaults to DEFAULT_VIDEO_FILE_SIZE_IN_MB)",
            "lineNumber": 256
          },
          {
            "text": "        chunk_size: Maximum number of files per chunk (defaults to DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE)",
            "lineNumber": 257
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 258
          },
          {
            "text": "    logging.info(\"Start aggregate_datasets\")",
            "lineNumber": 259
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 260
          },
          {
            "text": "    if data_files_size_in_mb is None:",
            "lineNumber": 261
          },
          {
            "text": "        data_files_size_in_mb = DEFAULT_DATA_FILE_SIZE_IN_MB",
            "lineNumber": 262
          },
          {
            "text": "    if video_files_size_in_mb is None:",
            "lineNumber": 263
          },
          {
            "text": "        video_files_size_in_mb = DEFAULT_VIDEO_FILE_SIZE_IN_MB",
            "lineNumber": 264
          },
          {
            "text": "    if chunk_size is None:",
            "lineNumber": 265
          },
          {
            "text": "        chunk_size = DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE",
            "lineNumber": 266
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 267
          },
          {
            "text": "    all_metadata = (",
            "lineNumber": 268
          },
          {
            "text": "        [LeRobotDatasetMetadata(repo_id) for repo_id in repo_ids]",
            "lineNumber": 269
          },
          {
            "text": "        if roots is None",
            "lineNumber": 270
          },
          {
            "text": "        else [",
            "lineNumber": 271
          },
          {
            "text": "            LeRobotDatasetMetadata(repo_id, root=root) for repo_id, root in zip(repo_ids, roots, strict=False)",
            "lineNumber": 272
          },
          {
            "text": "        ]",
            "lineNumber": 273
          },
          {
            "text": "    )",
            "lineNumber": 274
          },
          {
            "text": "    fps, robot_type, features = validate_all_metadata(all_metadata)",
            "lineNumber": 275
          },
          {
            "text": "    video_keys = [key for key in features if features[key][\"dtype\"] == \"video\"]",
            "lineNumber": 276
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 277
          },
          {
            "text": "    dst_meta = LeRobotDatasetMetadata.create",
            "lineNumber": 278
          }
        ]
      },

整理后的 Lerobot 项目分析如下：

---

# LeRobot 项目探索报告

## 1. 项目结构概览

### 主要目录

| 目录 | 用途 |
|------|------|
| `src/lerobot/` | 核心库 |
| `src/lerobot/configs/` | 训练 / 数据集 / 策略配置（Python dataclass） |
| `src/lerobot/datasets/` | LeRobot 数据集与加载逻辑 |
| `src/lerobot/policies/` | 策略实现（pi0, pi05, diffusion, act 等） |
| `src/lerobot/scripts/` | 入口脚本（`lerobot_train.py` 等） |
| `bt/sftpi05/` | PI0.5 SFT 示例脚本 |
| `examples/training/` | 示例训练脚本 |
| `tests/` | 测试 |

### 训练相关入口

- `lerobot-train`：对应 `src/lerobot/scripts/lerobot_train.py`
- 示例：`examples/training/train_policy.py`（Diffusion）、`bt/sftpi05/train_pi05.py`（PI0.5）

---

## 2. PI0 / PI0.5 训练配置

### 配置方式

项目使用 **Python dataclass** 和 draccus 解析 CLI，而不是 YAML 配置。策略配置主要在：

- `src/lerobot/configs/train.py`：`TrainPipelineConfig`（`dataset`, `policy`, `output_dir` 等）
- `src/lerobot/configs/default.py`：`DatasetConfig`（`repo_id`, `root`, `episodes` 等）
- `src/lerobot/configs/policies.py`：`PreTrainedConfig` 基类

### PI0.5 策略配置

```python
# src/lerobot/policies/pi05/configuration_pi05.py (摘要)
@PreTrainedConfig.register_subclass("pi05")
class PI05Config(PreTrainedConfig):
    paligemma_variant: str = "gemma_2b"
    action_expert_variant: str = "gemma_300m"
    chunk_size: int = 50
    n_obs_steps: int = 1
    max_state_dim: int = 32
    max_action_dim: int = 32
    # Flow matching, RTC, 训练/优化相关参数...
    def action_delta_indices(self) -> list:
        return list(range(self.chunk_size))  # [0..49]
    def observation_delta_indices(self) -> None:
        return None
```

### 官方 PI0.5 训练命令（docs 示例）

```bash
lerobot-train \
    --dataset.repo_id=your_dataset \
    --policy.type=pi05 \
    --output_dir=./outputs/pi05_training \
    --policy.repo_id=your_repo_id \
    --policy.pretrained_path=lerobot/pi05_base \
    --policy.compile_model=true \
    --policy.gradient_checkpointing=true \
    --policy.dtype=bfloat16 \
    --policy.freeze_vision_encoder=false \
    --steps=3000 \
    --batch_size=32
```

---

## 3. 训练脚本

### lerobot-train 流程

- 入口：`lerobot-train` → `lerobot.scripts.lerobot_train:main`
- 主逻辑：`src/lerobot/scripts/lerobot_train.py`

核心步骤：

1. `cfg.validate()`：校验并加载 `--policy.path` 等
2. `make_dataset(cfg)`：按 `cfg.dataset` 创建 `LeRobotDataset`（含 `delta_timestamps`）
3. `make_policy(cfg.policy, ds_meta=dataset.meta, ...)`：创建策略
4. `make_pre_post_processors(...)`：根据策略类型构建 pre/postprocessor
5. 主循环：取 batch → preprocessor → policy.forward → backward → 保存 checkpoint

### bt/sftpi05/train_pi05.py 示例

独立 PI0.5 微调脚本，流程与官方一致：

```python
# 数据集：LeRobot v3.0
ds_meta = LeRobotDatasetMetadata(args.repo_id, root=args.local_root)
delta_timestamps = resolve_delta_timestamps(PI05Config(), ds_meta)
dataset = LeRobotDataset(args.repo_id, root=..., episodes=..., delta_timestamps=delta_timestamps)

# 策略
policy = PI05Policy.from_pretrained(args.pretrained_path, config=config)

# 训练
for step in range(steps):
    batch = next(dl_iter)
    batch = preprocessor(batch)
    loss, _ = policy.forward(batch)
    loss.backward()
    optimizer.step()
```

---

## 4. LeRobot 数据集格式 v3.0

### 版本与布局

- 版本常量：`CODEBASE_VERSION = "v3.0"`（`src/lerobot/datasets/lerobot_dataset.py:82`）
- 目录结构示例：

```
dataset_root/
├── data/
│   └── chunk-{chunk_index:03d}/
│       └── file-{file_index:03d}.parquet
├── meta/
│   ├── info.json          # 元信息、features、fps
│   ├── stats.json         # 归一化统计
│   ├── tasks.parquet      # 任务文本
│   └── episodes/
│       └── chunk-000/
│           └── file-000.parquet
└── videos/                # 可选
    └── observation.images.{key}/
```

### info.json 示例（libero_v30）

```json
{
  "codebase_version": "v3.0",
  "fps": 10,
  "features": {
    "observation.images.top": {"dtype": "image", "shape": [3, 256, 256], ...},
    "observation.images.wrist": {...},
    "observation.state": {"dtype": "float32", "shape": [8], ...},
    "action": {"dtype": "float32", "shape": [7], ...},
    "timestamp": {...}, "frame_index": {...}, "episode_index": {...}
  },
  "data_path": "data/chunk-{chunk_index:03d}/file-{file_index:03d}.parquet"
}
```

### 核心类

- `LeRobotDatasetMetadata(repo_id, root, revision, ...)`：加载 `info.json`, `stats.json`, `episodes` 等
- `LeRobotDataset(repo_id, root, episodes, delta_timestamps, ...)`：主数据集类，内部依赖 `LeRobotDatasetMetadata`

---

## 5. bt/sftpi05 目录

### 文件说明

| 文件 | 作用 |
|------|------|
| `train_pi05.py` | PI0.5 SFT 主脚本，支持 v3.0 数据、HuggingFace Hub 和本地 root |
| `run.sh` | 封装：convert/debug/debug-dummy/train |
| `config.py` | `PI05SFTConfig` 配置类 |
| `gen_dummy_dataset.py` | 生成 v3.0 dummy 数据集（随机 state/image/action） |
| `convert_libero.py` | 将 physical-intelligence/libero v2.0 转为 v3.0 |
| `libero_dataset.py` | 旧版 Libero v2.0 适配器（目前 train_pi05 已改为用 LeRobotDataset） |
| `opusgen.md` | 相关对话 / 说明记录 |

### 运行示例

```bash
# 调试（2 episodes，随机权重，20 steps）
./bt/sftpi05/run.sh debug

# 用 dummy 数据快速验证
./bt/sftpi05/run.sh debug-dummy

# 正式微调
./bt/sftpi05/run.sh train

# 仅转换 libero 为 v3.0
./bt/sftpi05/run.sh convert [N]
```

## 7. PI0 / PI0.5 训练配置小结

### PI0.5 默认参数（`PI05Config`）

- `chunk_size=50`, `n_obs_steps=1`
- `action_delta_indices = list(range(50))`
- `observation_delta_indices = None`
- 归一化：`STATE` / `ACTION` 默认使用 `QUANTILES`；如数据集无 quantiles 可改为 `MEAN_STD`

### 数据与 delta_timestamps 工厂逻辑

```python
# src/lerobot/datasets/factory.py
def resolve_delta_timestamps(cfg, ds_meta):
    delta_timestamps = {}
    if cfg.action_delta_indices:
        delta_timestamps["action"] = [i/ds_meta.fps for i in cfg.action_delta_indices]
    if cfg.observation_delta_indices:
        for key in ds_meta.features:
            if key.startswith("observation."):
                delta_timestamps[key] = [i/ds_meta.fps for i in cfg.observation_delta_indices]
    return delta_timestamps or None
```

---

## 8. HuggingFace 社区数据集使用方式

### 加载方式

1. **仅 repo_id（从 Hub 下载）**

```python
dataset = LeRobotDataset("physical-intelligence/libero")
# 或
ds_meta = LeRobotDatasetMetadata("lerobot/pusht")
```

2. **本地缓存路径（root）**

```python
dataset = LeRobotDataset(
    "physical-intelligence/libero",
    root="data/libero_v30",
    episodes=[0, 1],
    delta_timestamps=delta_timestamps,
    download_videos=False,
)
```

3. **官方训练流程**

`make_dataset()` 会根据 `cfg.dataset.repo_id` 和 `cfg.dataset.root` 调用 `LeRobotDatasetMetadata` 和 `LeRobotDataset`，同时用 `cfg.policy` 的 `action_delta_indices` / `observation_delta_indices` 构造 `delta_timestamps`。

### 数据要求

- `codebase_version` 需为 v3.0；旧版可用 `src/lerobot/datasets/v30/convert_dataset_v21_to_v30.py` 转换
- 需要 `meta/info.json`、`meta/stats.json`、`meta/episodes/` 等 meta 文件

### community_dataset_v3

- 代码库中未发现 `community_dataset_v3` 字符串
- 凡符合 LeRobot v3.0 格式的 HF 数据集，均可通过 `LeRobotDataset(repo_id)` 或 `LeRobotDataset(repo_id, root=local_path)` 使用

          {
            "lineNumber": 600
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 601
          },
          {
            "text": "        2. Your dataset doesn't already exists (either on local disk or on the Hub): you can create an empty",

          {

            "lineNumber": 605

          {
            "lineNumber": 606
          },
          {
            "text": "        In terms of files, LeRobotDataset encapsulates 3 main things:",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 658
          },
          {
            "text": "        Note that this file-based structure is designed to be as versatile as possible. Multiple episodes are",
            "lineNumber": 659
          },
          {
            "text": "        consolidated into chunked files which improves storage efficiency and loading performance. The",
            "lineNumber": 660
          },
          {
            "text": "        structure of the dataset is entirely described in the info.json file, which can be easily downloaded",
            "lineNumber": 661
          },
          {
            "text": "        or viewed directly on the hub before downloading any actual data. The type of files used are very",
            "lineNumber": 662
          },
          {
            "text": "        simple and do not need complex tools to be read, it only uses .parquet, .json and .mp4 files (and .md",
            "lineNumber": 663
          },
          {
            "text": "        for the README).",
            "lineNumber": 664
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 665
          },
          {
            "text": "        Args:",
            "lineNumber": 666
          },
          {
            "text": "            repo_id (str): This is the repo id that will be used to fetch the dataset.",
            "lineNumber": 667
          },
          {
            "text": "            root (Path | None, optional): Local directory where the dataset will be downloaded and",
            "lineNumber": 668

          {
            "text": "                stored. If set, all dataset files will be stored directly under this path. If not set, the",
            "lineNumber": 669

            "lineNumber": 670
          },

            "lineNumber": 671
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 672

            "lineNumber": 673
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 674

            "lineNumber": 677

            "lineNumber": 684

            "lineNumber": 692

          {

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 901
          },

            "lineNumber": 904
          },
          {
            "text": "        # Get available episode indices from cached dataset",

            "lineNumber": 910

            "text": "class MultiLeRobotDataset(torch.utils.data.Dataset):",

          },

          },
          {

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "text": "        self.root = Path(root) if root else HF_LEROBOT_HOME",
            "lineNumber": 1742
          },
          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;div align=\"center\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;img src=\"./media/readme/robots_control_video.webp\" width=\"640px\" alt=\"Reachy 2 Demo\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;/div&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "LeRobot provides a unified &#96;Robot&#96; class interface that decouples control logic from hardware specifics. It supports a wide range of robots and teleoperation devices.",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;python",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "from lerobot.robots.myrobot import MyRobot",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "# Connect to a robot",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "robot = MyRobot(config=...)",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "robot.connect()",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "# Read observation and send action",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "obs = robot.get_observation()",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "action = model.select_action(obs)",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "robot.send_action(action)",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "**Supported Hardware:** SO100, LeKiwi, Koch, HopeJR, OMX, EarthRover, Reachy2, Gamepads, Keyboards, Phones, OpenARM, Unitree G1.",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "While these devices are natively integrated into the LeRobot codebase, the library is designed to be extensible. You can easily implement the Robot interface to utilize LeRobot's data collection, training, and visualization tools for your own custom robot.",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "For detailed hardware setup guides, see the [Hardware Documentation](https://huggingface.co/docs/lerobot/integrate_hardware).",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "## LeRobot Dataset",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "To solve the data fragmentation problem in robotics, we utilize the **LeRobotDataset** format.",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Structure:** Synchronized MP4 videos (or images) for vision and Parquet files for state/action data.",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "- **HF Hub Integration:** Explore thousands of robotics datasets on the [Hugging Face Hub](https://huggingface.co/lerobot).",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Tools:** Seamlessly delete episodes, split by indices/fractions, add/remove features, and merge multiple datasets.",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;python",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "from lerobot.datasets.lerobot_dataset import LeRobotDataset",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "# Load a dataset from the Hub",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "text": "dataset = LeRobotDataset(\"lerobot/aloha_mobile_cabinet\")",
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "text": "# Access data (automatically handles video decoding)",
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "episode_index=0",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "print(f\"{dataset[episode_index]['action'].shape=}\\n\")",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "Learn more about it in the [LeRobotDataset Documentation](https://huggingface.co/docs/lerobot/lerobot-dataset-v3)",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "## SoTA Models",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "LeRobot implements state-of-the-art policies in pure PyTorch, covering Imitation Learning, Reinforcement Learning, and Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, with more coming soon. It also provides you with the tools to instrument and inspect your training process.",
            "lineNumber": 89
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.4686746597290039
    },

            "lineNumber": 959
          },

          },

          },

          },
          {

          {

          },
          {
            "text": "    def __getitem__(self, idx) -&gt; dict:",

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 1089

          },

          },

          },
          {
            "text": "            for key, val in query_result.items():",

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1097
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 1098
          },
          {
            "text": "            current_ts = item[\"timestamp\"].item()",

          },

          {

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1103
          },
          {

          },

          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "This script demonstrates the use of &#96;LeRobotDataset&#96; class for handling and processing robotic datasets from Hugging Face.",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "It illustrates how to load datasets, manipulate them, and apply transformations suitable for machine learning tasks in PyTorch.",

            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "Features included in this script:",

            "lineNumber": 20
          },

            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "- Accessing frames by episode number.",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "- Using advanced dataset features like timestamp-based frame selection.",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "- Demonstrating compatibility with PyTorch DataLoader for batch processing.",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },

          {
            "text": "def get_hf_features_from_features(features: dict) -&gt; datasets.Features:",
            "lineNumber": 563,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Convert a LeRobot features dictionary to a &#96;datasets.Features&#96; object.",
            "lineNumber": 564
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 565
          },
          {
            "text": "    Args:",
            "lineNumber": 566
          },
          {
            "text": "        features (dict): A LeRobot-style feature dictionary.",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 568
          },
          {
            "text": "    Returns:",
            "lineNumber": 569
          },
          {
            "text": "        datasets.Features: The corresponding Hugging Face &#96;datasets.Features&#96; object.",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 571
          },
          {
            "text": "    Raises:",
            "lineNumber": 572
          },
          {
            "text": "        ValueError: If a feature has an unsupported shape.",
            "lineNumber": 573
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 574
          },
          {
            "text": "    hf_features = {}",
            "lineNumber": 575
          },
          {
            "text": "    for key, ft in features.items():",
            "lineNumber": 576
          },
          {
            "text": "        if ft[\"dtype\"] == \"video\":",
            "lineNumber": 577
          },
          {
            "text": "            continue",
            "lineNumber": 578
          },
          {
            "text": "        elif ft[\"dtype\"] == \"image\":",
            "lineNumber": 579
          },
          {
            "text": "            hf_features[key] = datasets.Image()",
            "lineNumber": 580
          },
          {
            "text": "        elif ft[\"shape\"] == (1,):",
            "lineNumber": 581
          },
          {
            "text": "            hf_features[key] = datasets.Value(dtype=ft[\"dtype\"])",
            "lineNumber": 582
          },
          {
            "text": "        elif len(ft[\"shape\"]) == 1:",
            "lineNumber": 583
          },
          {
            "text": "            hf_features[key] = datasets.Sequence(",
            "lineNumber": 584
          },
          {
            "text": "                length=ft[\"shape\"][0], feature=datasets.Value(dtype=ft[\"dtype\"])",
            "lineNumber": 585
          },
          {
            "text": "            )",
            "lineNumber": 586
          },
          {
            "text": "        elif len(ft[\"shape\"]) == 2:",
            "lineNumber": 587
          },
          {
            "text": "            hf_features[key] = datasets.Array2D(shape=ft[\"shape\"], dtype=ft[\"dtype\"])",
            "lineNumber": 588
          },
          {
            "text": "        elif len(ft[\"shape\"]) == 3:",
            "lineNumber": 589
          },
          {
            "text": "            hf_features[key] = datasets.Array3D(shape=ft[\"shape\"], dtype=ft[\"dtype\"])",
            "lineNumber": 590
          },
          {
            "text": "        elif len(ft[\"shape\"]) == 4:",
            "lineNumber": 591
          },
          {
            "text": "            hf_features[key] = datasets.Array4D(shape=ft[\"shape\"], dtype=ft[\"dtype\"])",
            "lineNumber": 592
          },
          {
            "text": "        elif len(ft[\"shape\"]) == 5:",
            "lineNumber": 593
          },
          {
            "text": "            hf_features[key] = datasets.Array5D(shape=ft[\"shape\"], dtype=ft[\"dtype\"])",
            "lineNumber": 594
          },
          {
            "text": "        else:",
            "lineNumber": 595
          },
          {

          },
          {

          {
            "text": "class ReplayBuffer:",
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "def to_lerobot_dataset(",
            "lineNumber": 509
          },
          {
            "text": "        self,",
            "lineNumber": 510
          },
          {
            "text": "        repo_id: str,",
            "lineNumber": 511
          },
          {
            "text": "        fps=1,",
            "lineNumber": 512
          },
          {
            "text": "        root=None,",
            "lineNumber": 513
          },
          {
            "text": "        task_name=\"from_replay_buffer\",",
            "lineNumber": 514
          },
          {
            "text": "    ) -&gt; LeRobotDataset:",
            "lineNumber": 515
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 553
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 554
          },
          {
            "text": "        # Create an empty LeRobotDataset",
            "lineNumber": 555
          },
          {
            "text": "        lerobot_dataset = LeRobotDataset.create(",
            "lineNumber": 556
          },
          {
            "text": "            repo_id=repo_id,",
            "lineNumber": 557
          },
          {
            "text": "            fps=fps,",
            "lineNumber": 558
          },
          {
            "text": "            root=root,",
            "lineNumber": 559
          },
          {
            "text": "            robot_type=None,",
            "lineNumber": 560
          },
          {
            "text": "            features=features,",
            "lineNumber": 561
          },
          {
            "text": "            use_videos=True,",
            "lineNumber": 562
          },
          {
            "text": "        )",
            "lineNumber": 563
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 564
          },
          {
            "text": "        # Start writing images if needed",
            "lineNumber": 565
          },
          {
            "text": "        lerobot_dataset.start_image_writer(num_processes=0, num_threads=3)",
            "lineNumber": 566
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 567
          },
          {
            "text": "        # Convert transitions into episodes and frames",
            "lineNumber": 568
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 569
          },
          {
            "text": "        for idx in range(self.size):",
            "lineNumber": 570
          },
          {
            "text": "            actual_idx = (self.position - self.size + idx) % self.capacity",
            "lineNumber": 571
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 572
          },
          {
            "text": "            frame_dict = {}",
            "lineNumber": 573
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 574
          },
          {
            "text": "            # Fill the data for state keys",
            "lineNumber": 575
          },
          {
            "text": "            for key in self.states:",
            "lineNumber": 576
          },
          {
            "text": "                frame_dict[key] = self.states[key][actual_idx].cpu()",
            "lineNumber": 577
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 578
          },
          {
            "text": "            # Fill action, reward, done",
            "lineNumber": 579
          },
          {
            "text": "            frame_dict[ACTION] = self.actions[actual_idx].cpu()",
            "lineNumber": 580
          },
          {
            "text": "            frame_dict[REWARD] = torch.tensor([self.rewards[actual_idx]], dtype=torch.float32).cpu()",
            "lineNumber": 581
          },
          {
            "text": "            frame_dict[DONE] = torch.tensor([self.dones[actual_idx]], dtype=torch.bool).cpu()",
            "lineNumber": 582
          },
          {
            "text": "            frame_dict[\"task\"] = task_name",
            "lineNumber": 583
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 584
          },
          {
            "text": "            # Add complementary_info if available",
            "lineNumber": 585
          },
          {
            "text": "            if self.has_complementary_info:",
            "lineNumber": 586
          },
          {
            "text": "                for key in self.complementary_info_keys:",
            "lineNumber": 587
          },
          {
            "text": "                    val = self.complementary_info[key][actual_idx]",
            "lineNumber": 588
          },
          {
            "text": "                    # Convert tensors to CPU",
            "lineNumber": 589
          },
          {
            "text": "                    if isinstance(val, torch.Tensor):",
            "lineNumber": 590
          },
          {
            "text": "                        if val.ndim == 0:",
            "lineNumber": 591
          },
          {
            "text": "                            val = val.unsqueeze(0)",
            "lineNumber": 592
          },
          {
            "text": "                        frame_dict[f\"complementary_info.{key}\"] = val.cpu()",
            "lineNumber": 593
          },
          {
            "text": "                    # Non-tensor values can be used directly",
            "lineNumber": 594
          },
          {
            "text": "                    else:",
            "lineNumber": 595
          },
          {
            "text": "                        frame_dict[f\"complementary_info.{key}\"] = val",
            "lineNumber": 596
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 597
          },
          {
            "text": "            # Add to the dataset's buffer",
            "lineNumber": 598
          },
          {
            "text": "            lerobot_dataset.add_frame(frame_dict)",
            "lineNumber": 599
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 600
          },
          {
            "text": "            # If we reached an episode boundary, call save_episode, reset counters",
            "lineNumber": 601
          },
          {
            "text": "            if self.dones[actual_idx] or self.truncateds[actual_idx]:",
            "lineNumber": 602
          },
          {
            "text": "                lerobot_dataset.save_episode()",
            "lineNumber": 603
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 604
          },
          {
            "text": "        # Save any remaining frames in the buffer",
            "lineNumber": 605
          }
        ]
      },

      "codeBlock": {

        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "def convert_lerobot_dataset_to_cropped_lerobot_dataset(",
            "lineNumber": 163,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    original_dataset: LeRobotDataset,",
            "lineNumber": 164,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    crop_params_dict: dict[str, tuple[int, int, int, int]],",
            "lineNumber": 165,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    new_repo_id: str,",
            "lineNumber": 166,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    new_dataset_root: str,",
            "lineNumber": 167,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    resize_size: tuple[int, int] = (128, 128),",
            "lineNumber": 168,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    push_to_hub: bool = False,",
            "lineNumber": 169,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    task: str = \"\",",
            "lineNumber": 170,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": ") -&gt; LeRobotDataset:",
            "lineNumber": 171,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "text": "    Converts an existing LeRobotDataset by iterating over its episodes and frames,",
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "text": "    applying cropping and resizing to image observations, and saving a new dataset",
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "text": "    with the transformed data.",
            "lineNumber": 175
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176
          },
          {
            "text": "    Args:",
            "lineNumber": 177
          },
          {
            "text": "        original_dataset (LeRobotDataset): The source dataset.",
            "lineNumber": 178
          },
          {
            "text": "        crop_params_dict (Dict[str, Tuple[int, int, int, int]]):",
            "lineNumber": 179
          },
          {
            "text": "            A dictionary mapping observation keys to crop parameters (top, left, height, width).",
            "lineNumber": 180
          },
          {
            "text": "        new_repo_id (str): Repository id for the new dataset.",
            "lineNumber": 181
          },
          {
            "text": "        new_dataset_root (str): The root directory where the new dataset will be written.",
            "lineNumber": 182
          },
          {
            "text": "        resize_size (Tuple[int, int], optional): The target size (height, width) after cropping.",
            "lineNumber": 183
          },
          {
            "text": "            Defaults to (128, 128).",
            "lineNumber": 184
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 185
          },
          {
            "text": "    Returns:",
            "lineNumber": 186
          },
          {
            "text": "        LeRobotDataset: A new LeRobotDataset where the specified image observations have been cropped",
            "lineNumber": 187
          },
          {
            "text": "                        and resized.",
            "lineNumber": 188
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 189
          },
          {
            "text": "    # 1. Create a new (empty) LeRobotDataset for writing.",
            "lineNumber": 190
          },
          {
            "text": "    new_dataset = LeRobotDataset.create(",
            "lineNumber": 191
          },
          {
            "text": "        repo_id=new_repo_id,",
            "lineNumber": 192
          },
          {
            "text": "        fps=int(original_dataset.fps),",
            "lineNumber": 193
          },
          {
            "text": "        root=new_dataset_root,",
            "lineNumber": 194
          },
          {
            "text": "        robot_type=original_dataset.meta.robot_type,",
            "lineNumber": 195
          },
          {
            "text": "        features=original_dataset.meta.info[\"features\"],",
            "lineNumber": 196
          },
          {
            "text": "        use_videos=len(original_dataset.meta.video_keys) &gt; 0,",
            "lineNumber": 197
          },
          {
            "text": "    )",
            "lineNumber": 198
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Update the metadata for every image key that will be cropped:",
            "lineNumber": 200
          },
          {
            "text": "    # (Here we simply set the shape to be the final resize_size.)",
            "lineNumber": 201
          },
          {
            "text": "    for key in crop_params_dict:",
            "lineNumber": 202
          },
          {
            "text": "        if key in new_dataset.meta.info[\"features\"]:",
            "lineNumber": 203
          },
          {
            "text": "            new_dataset.meta.info[\"features\"][key][\"shape\"] = [3] + list(resize_size)",
            "lineNumber": 204
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205
          },
          {
            "text": "    # TODO:  Directly modify the mp4 video + meta info features, instead of recreating a dataset",
            "lineNumber": 206
          },
          {
            "text": "    prev_episode_index = 0",
            "lineNumber": 207
          },
          {
            "text": "    for frame_idx in tqdm(range(len(original_dataset))):",
            "lineNumber": 208
          },
          {
            "text": "        frame = original_dataset[frame_idx]",
            "lineNumber": 209
          }
        ]
      },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/source/lerobot-dataset-v3.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 34
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# LeRobotDataset v3.0",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;LeRobotDataset v3.0&#96; is a standardized format for robot learning data. It provides unified access to multi-modal time-series data, sensorimotor signals and multi‑camera video, as well as rich metadata for indexing, search, and visualization on the Hugging Face Hub.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "This docs will guide you to:",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "- Understand the v3.0 design and directory layout",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "- Record a dataset and push it to the Hub",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "- Load datasets for training with &#96;LeRobotDataset&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "- Stream datasets without downloading using &#96;StreamingLeRobotDataset&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "- Apply image transforms for data augmentation during training",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "- Migrate existing &#96;v2.1&#96; datasets to &#96;v3.0&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "## What’s new in &#96;v3&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "- **File-based storage**: Many episodes per Parquet/MP4 file (v2 used one file per episode).",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Relational metadata**: Episode boundaries and lookups are resolved through metadata, not filenames.",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Hub-native streaming**: Consume datasets directly from the Hub with &#96;StreamingLeRobotDataset&#96;.",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Lower file-system pressure**: Fewer, larger files ⇒ faster initialization and fewer issues at scale.",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Unified organization**: Clean directory layout with consistent path templates across data and videos.",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "## Installation",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;LeRobotDataset v3.0&#96; will be included in &#96;lerobot &gt;= 0.4.0&#96;.",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "Until that stable release, you can use the main branch by following the [build from source instructions](./installation#from-source).",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "## Record a dataset",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "Run the command below to record a dataset with the SO-101 and push to the Hub:",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "lerobot-record \\",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "  --robot.type=so101_follower \\",
            "lineNumber": 34
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.40340641140937805
    },

          {
            "text": "class ReplayBuffer:",
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "isSignature": true
          },

            "lineNumber": 412
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 413
          },
          {
            "text": "    @classmethod",
            "lineNumber": 414
          },
          {
            "text": "    def from_lerobot_dataset(",
            "lineNumber": 415
          },
          {
            "text": "        cls,",
            "lineNumber": 416
          },
          {
            "text": "        lerobot_dataset: LeRobotDataset,",
            "lineNumber": 417
          },
          {
            "text": "        device: str = \"cuda:0\",",
            "lineNumber": 418
          },
          {
            "text": "        state_keys: Sequence[str] | None = None,",
            "lineNumber": 419
          },
          {
            "text": "        capacity: int | None = None,",
            "lineNumber": 420
          },
          {
            "text": "        image_augmentation_function: Callable | None = None,",
            "lineNumber": 421
          },
          {
            "text": "        use_drq: bool = True,",
            "lineNumber": 422
          },
          {
            "text": "        storage_device: str = \"cpu\",",
            "lineNumber": 423
          },
          {
            "text": "        optimize_memory: bool = False,",
            "lineNumber": 424
          },
          {
            "text": "    ) -&gt; \"ReplayBuffer\":",
            "lineNumber": 425
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 426
          },
          {
            "text": "        Convert a LeRobotDataset into a ReplayBuffer.",
            "lineNumber": 427
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 428
          },
          {
            "text": "        Args:",
            "lineNumber": 429
          },
          {
            "text": "            lerobot_dataset (LeRobotDataset): The dataset to convert.",
            "lineNumber": 430
          },
          {
            "text": "            device (str): The device for sampling tensors. Defaults to \"cuda:0\".",
            "lineNumber": 431
